system.name: Kinetic Gain Sepsis Early Warning System
system.description: Continuous-monitoring AI system surfacing high-risk-of-sepsis patients to clinicians. Designed to operate alongside (not replace) the bedside team. SaMD class II under 21st Century Cures Act CDS exclusion criteria; clinician override required on every recommendation.

clinical_context.indication: Early detection of adult inpatient sepsis using vital signs, lab values, and nursing documentation in real time. Surfaces a risk score 6-12 hours before traditional EWS triggers.
clinical_context.intended_use: Clinical decision support for adult inpatient providers to identify patients at elevated risk of sepsis onset. Output is a risk score and supporting feature attribution; the system does not initiate treatment.

regulatory.samd_classification_rationale: Healthcare situation: serious (sepsis). Healthcare decision: drive clinical management (alert clinician to investigate). Per IMDRF framework: Class II.

validation_studies.title: Multi-site prospective validation of Kinetic Gain Sepsis EWS across 4 US academic centers
validation_studies.primary_outcome: Detection of sepsis onset (Sepsis-3 criteria) at least 6 hours before standard MEWS trigger
validation_studies.results_summary: Achieved sensitivity 0.84, specificity 0.78, AUC 0.89. Sensitivity remained >0.80 across age, sex, and self-reported race subgroups. Median lead time vs MEWS: 8.7 hours.

validation_studies.title: Real-world post-market surveillance after 12 months of deployment
validation_studies.primary_outcome: False-positive rate per shift and alert-acknowledgment timing
validation_studies.results_summary: Real-world FPR was 0.21 (vs 0.22 in validation); median acknowledgment time 4.2 minutes; no critical incidents reported during the window.

training_data_sources: Internal multi-site dataset N=287k encounters across 9 US health systems (2018-2023)

performance_metrics.measurement_population: Adult inpatient encounters at 4 US academic centers (validation cohort, 2024-2025)

escalation_protocols: Critical alerts route to charge RN AND attending; non-acknowledgment within 15 min escalates to rapid response team.
All alert dispositions are logged with clinician identity, timestamp, and free-text rationale.
